retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Equipment -- Resin-based sealants
retrieved_docs.content: Based on their polymerization mechanism, resin sealants are available in either self-curing (auto-curing) or light-curing formulations. Self-cure sealants harden through a chemical reaction initiated by tertiary amines and benzoyl peroxide, whereas light-cure sealants utilize visible light to activate photo-initiators, providing a longer working time and more controlled placement. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Benzoyl Peroxide -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the indications for benzoyl peroxide use. Evaluate the mechanism of action of benzoyl peroxide. Identify the most common adverse effects associated with benzoyl peroxide use. Implement effective collaboration and communication among interprofessional team members to improve outcomes and treatment efficacy for patients who might benefit from benzoyl peroxide treatment. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Benzoyl Peroxide -- Monitoring
retrieved_docs.content: No monitoring parameters were identified for benzoyl peroxide, and no routine tests are recommended. The development of contact dermatitis to benzoyl peroxide should be monitored, with discontinuation of the medication in patients who develop marked erythema and irritation with its use. Before using benzoyl peroxide, a test area should have a test treatment to monitor for tolerability and sensitivity. [20] [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Benzoyl Peroxide -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: Patients who present with a known history of hypersensitivity to benzoyl peroxide should not use benzoyl peroxide products. Patients with hypersensitivity to cinnamon and other benzoic acid derivatives should be cautious when using benzoyl peroxide, as cross-reactivity may occur. [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Benzoyl Peroxide -- Contraindications -- Warning and Precautions
retrieved_docs.content: Benzoyl peroxide is not indicated for use around the eyes, nose, mouth, mucous membranes, and open skin, as it may cause severe irritation. For mucous membrane or ocular contact, thoroughly rinse affected areas with water for a minimum of 15 minutes. Patients should minimize UV exposure and apply sunscreen to reduce the risk of developing phototoxicity and skin irritation. [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Preventing Cross Infection in the Dental Office -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: This method uses conventional dry heat ovens with a short cycle and high temperatures for items that cannot be exposed to moist heat, static air, or forced air. The high temperatures may damage heat-sensitive items; cycles are longer at lower temperatures, and ovens must be calibrated. Dry heat sterilization does not corrode instruments, and the effectiveness of sterilization can be verified. Industrial hot air ovens can sterilize more instruments at the same time. [1] Unsaturated chemical vapor pressure sterilization

retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Technique or Treatment -- Light Cured Sealant
retrieved_docs.content: The tip of the curing light should be positioned 3 to 5 mm from the sealant surface and applied for 10 to 20 seconds to ensure proper polymerization. Maintaining the correct distance and curing time enhances the strength and longevity of the material.

retrieved_docs.title: Benzoyl Peroxide -- Administration -- Specific Patient Populations
retrieved_docs.content: Breastfeeding considerations: There is insufficient data on using benzoyl peroxide while breastfeeding. Because of its low rate of systemic absorption, minimal risk to the infant is likely, and the topical use of benzoyl peroxide is generally considered safe while breastfeeding, as long as its application is not where the infant would have direct contact during breastfeeding or close skin-on-skin contact. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Preventing Cross Infection in the Dental Office -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: This method uses an unsaturated chemical vapor system of alcohol and formaldehyde. The cycles are short, and the load comes out dry; the effectiveness of the sterilization can be verified, and corrosion-sensitive instruments do not rust. The disadvantage of this method is that instruments must be completely dry beforehand, and heavy cloth wrappings of surgical instruments may not be penetrated. [1] Ethylene oxide sterilization

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanism of Setting
retrieved_docs.content: Setting occurs by light-induced polymerization. The acid-base setting mechanism does not exist due to the absence of polyacids from the composition.

retrieved_docs.content: Allantoin

retrieved_docs.title: Benzoyl Peroxide -- Adverse Effects
retrieved_docs.content: The most common adverse effect of benzoyl peroxide is its bleaching quality, potentially causing the discoloration of colored fabrics and hair bleaching. At higher concentrations, topical benzoyl peroxide causes mild dryness, erythema, and scaling. [20] There are also case reports of contact dermatitis in a small percentage of patients, which should arouse suspicion in patients who develop marked erythema and irritation at the treatment site. In such instances, the prudent course of action is stopping benzoyl peroxide.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 422
retrieved_docs.content: Peroxide anti‑acne preparations for topical use are classified within the broader chemical group peroxidy, and peroxidy itself is a subcategory of the larger family oxidy. This hierarchical relationship indicates that these dermatologic agents belong to the peroxidy class, which derives from the oxidy parent group, informing their chemical properties and therapeutic considerations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 4 -- Community 2744
retrieved_docs.content: Benzoyl peroxide is classified as a dermatological substance (dermatologické látky) and chemically belongs to the broader group of carboxylic acids and salts. This dual categorization highlights that benzoyl peroxide functions both as a therapeutic agent used in skin‑care treatments and as a member of the carboxylic acid/salt chemical family, linking its pharmacologic action to its underlying chemical structure. Understanding that benzoyl peroxide is a dermatological agent derived from carboxylic acids and salts aids clinicians in selecting appropriate formulations, anticipating its mode of action, and managing potential side effects within dermatologic patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2728
retrieved_docs.content: Benzoyl peroxide and methotrexate are both classified as dermatological substances, indicating that each is used in the treatment of skin conditions. As members of the same therapeutic category, these agents share relevance in dermatology, where they are employed for their specific pharmacologic actions on the skin.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1663
retrieved_docs.content: Peroxide anti‑acne preparations for topical use are classified within the broader chemical group of peroxidy, indicating that these acne‑treating agents belong to the larger family of peroxide compounds. Within the peroxidy family, prostaglandinové endoperoxidy represent a specific subclass, linking the pharmacologic use of peroxide‑based acne treatments to the biologically active prostaglandin endoperoxides. Recognizing that topical peroxide anti‑acne products are part of the peroxidy category, which also encompasses prostaglandinové endoperoxidy, helps clinicians appreciate the chemical relationships that underlie both therapeutic and physiological effects of these agents.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1465
retrieved_docs.content: Antiinfectives and antiseptics for local oral treatment are classified as members of the peroxid vodíku (hydrogen peroxide) group, indicating that these oral antimicrobial agents are formulated on the basis of hydrogen peroxide and are used for topical infection control within the mouth. This relationship underscores hydrogen peroxide’s role as the active component in such local oral antiinfective and antiseptic preparations.
